When generating code in this repo, follow these rules. The full conventions, architecture, and common tasks are in CLAUDE.md (read it for non-trivial work).

<!-- GENERATED by /generate-copilot from CLAUDE.md. Pre-/bootstrap: rules reflect docs/defaults.md greenfield defaults; regenerate after /bootstrap populates Conventions. -->

## Architecture
- Standalone components by default; NgModules only where the codebase hasn't migrated.
- Use `inject()` for DI in new code; don't mix constructor and inject() in one file.
- Feature areas are lazy-loaded routes; justify any eager load.
- Barrel files (`index.ts`) only at feature boundaries, never inside feature folders.

## SOLID
- DIP: every injected service depends on an abstraction — `abstract class` token (`{ provide: Foo, useClass: FooImpl }`) or `InjectionToken<T>`; never `new` a concrete service.
- Data carriers (models, DTOs, enums) get no abstraction.
- SRP: no god components/services; honour the smart/dumb split.
- OCP: at the third arm of a switch/if over a type code, replace with polymorphism.
- LSP: no `throw new Error('not implemented')`; fulfil the full contract.
- ISP: small role-based contracts, not one fat service interface.

## Component Design
- `ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush` on every new component.
- Smart components orchestrate state; dumb components use `@Input`/`@Output` only.
- Templates stay lean — no complex expressions or business logic; move to class or pipe.
- New control flow (`@if`, `@for`, `@switch`) in new code.
- Prefer signals over getter-based reactive state.

## State Management
- Local state: signals or simple properties. Shared state: the project's store approach — don't mix.
- No prop drilling past 2 component levels — use a service or store.
- Server state: handle loading, error, and success explicitly.

## RxJS
- Prefer `async` pipe over manual `.subscribe()`; manual subscribes need explicit cleanup.
- Cleanup via `takeUntilDestroyed(this.destroyRef)`; no manual ngOnDestroy subject patterns.
- No nested subscribes — use switchMap/mergeMap/concatMap/exhaustMap appropriately.
- Error handling in every stream; `catchError` only to recover, never to silence.

## API / HTTP
- One service per backend resource; all HTTP return types are typed interfaces — no `any`.
- Interceptors for cross-cutting concerns: auth tokens, errors, retry, loading.
- Environment config for API URLs; no hardcoded URLs.

## Typing
- `strict: true`; no overrides weakening strictness.
- No `any` — use `unknown` then narrow. No `as` assertions without a comment explaining why.
- Interfaces for data shapes; classes only when behavior is needed.

## Styling
- Encapsulated component styles (`ViewEncapsulation.Emulated`); no `None` without justification.
- `:host` for component-level styling; global styles only in `styles.scss`.

## Testing
- Test naming: `should [expected behavior] when [condition]`; TestBed + harnesses; HTTP via `provideHttpClientTesting`.
- Test behavior, not implementation; no tautological assertions; no over-mocking.
- No `fdescribe`/`fit`/`xdescribe`/`xit` committed; tests deterministic and hermetic.

## Boy Scout (always apply on touched files)
1. Replace manual ngOnDestroy subscription cleanup with `takeUntilDestroyed()`.
2. Replace nested `.subscribe()` with the appropriate RxJS operator.
3. Replace `any` with proper types.
4. Remove unused TypeScript and RxJS operator imports.
5. Remove commented-out code or template blocks (more than 1 line).
6. Remove unreferenced private fields, methods, or locals that tsc/lint flags.
7. Remove unused `@Input`/`@Output` properties.
